title: STARLINK-1234 vs STARLINK-5678 (Active/Active)
description: Two active operational Starlink satellites in intersecting planes negotiate lane priority based on fuel budget and mission priority.

agentA_proposals.justification: Standard operational satellite. Proposing a clean prograde burn of 0.31 m/s to raise apogee, shifting TCA crossing time and increasing miss distance to 47.3 km.
Standard operational satellite. Proposing a clean retrograde burn of 0.45 m/s to lower perigee, allowing the threat to pass overhead safely.
Cross-track normal burn to adjust orbital inclination slightly. Highly fuel-intensive but effective.

agentB_proposals.justification: Priority 8 active satellite. Proposing a minimal prograde burn of 0.25 m/s to raise apogee, shifting TCA crossing time and increasing miss distance to 38.5 km.
Priority 8 active satellite. Proposing a minimal retrograde burn of 0.38 m/s to lower perigee, allowing the threat to pass overhead safely.
Cross-track normal burn to adjust orbital inclination slightly. Highly fuel-intensive but effective.

satA_action.justification: Standard operational satellite. Proposing a clean prograde burn of 0.31 m/s to raise apogee, shifting TCA crossing time and increasing miss distance to 47.3 km.

satB_action.justification: Priority 8 active satellite. Proposing a minimal prograde burn of 0.25 m/s to raise apogee, shifting TCA crossing time and increasing miss distance to 38.5 km.

brief:
CONJUNCTION ALERT — HIGH RISK
Objects: STARLINK-1234 and STARLINK-5678
TCA: 2026-06-09T14:58:47.736343+00:00
Current Miss Distance: 1.209 km — BELOW SAFE THRESHOLD

Recommended Action: Both STARLINK-1234 and STARLINK-5678 will execute coordinated maneuvers.
Maneuver Details: STARLINK-1234: prograde burn (0.31 m/s)
STARLINK-5678: prograde burn (0.25 m/s) | Fuel Cost: 13.0% of reserve
New Miss Distance: 85.8 km — SAFE

Maneuver Justification:
The automated collision avoidance system has calculated an optimal avoidance strategy. A coordinated multi-satellite burn is optimal to split the fuel burden and maximize separation. This will increase the closest approach miss distance from 1.209 km to 85.8 km, which is well above the safety threshold of 10.0 km, minimizing the probability of collision while conserving spacecraft fuel reserves.
